prediction: At least 1 of the 8 COVER posts will show a price/availability rich snippet eligibility in GSC Enhancements panel by 2026-05-21
rationale: Product schema added to all 8 posts (commit ccf0426) matches the schema stack of #1 SERP result (Good Housekeeping). If Google reads but doesn't promote, the gap is likely missing offer/availability detail or duplicate content cluster signal.
action_taken: Added Product+Review+Person schema to all 8 posts via /tmp/inject_product_schema.py

prediction: At least 3 items from improvement_queue.md will be shipped by 2026-05-14 if the daily loop runs
rationale: Queue has 11 bounded items; user committed to running 1x/day. 3 items in 7 days = ~50% of total bandwidth. If <3 ship, the loop isn't running OR I'm overestimating my per-session throughput OR I keep getting distracted by non-queue work.
action_taken: Created social/improvement_queue.md with stage-rules; loop prompt drafted

prediction: GSC will show ≥1 organic impression on at least 1 of 8 COVER posts by 2026-06-05 (Stage-1 evaluation)
rationale: Sitemap accepted, 9 pages discovered. Sandbox effect notwithstanding, exact-match long-tail keywords on a freshly-indexed domain typically catch some long-tail impression within 30 days. 0 impressions would mean either (a) the cluster has no real search demand, or (b) Google is suppressing the cluster as low-quality near-duplicates.

prediction: By 2026-06-05 GSC 'Pages' report will show all 8 COVER posts indexed (vs whatever subset is indexed today), AND average crawl depth per post (Coverage > Crawl stats) will increase measurably over the prior 30 days. Mechanism: 24 fresh internal edges give Googlebot 3x more discovery paths into each post.
rationale: Internal linking is the highest-leverage indexing accelerator on a sandboxed new domain. If indexing coverage does NOT improve despite 24 new edges, the bottleneck is content-quality classification, not discovery — which would invalidate further on-page polishing and force a Stage-1 cluster pivot regardless of impression count.
action_taken: Injected balanced ring-link Related Reviews block (i+1, i+3, i+5 mod 8) across all 8 COVER posts via /tmp/inject_related_reviews.py

prediction: Adding og:image + Twitter Card tags will NOT measurably change GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05 because the bottleneck is sandbox/discovery, not social-share CTR (we have ~0 shares). Specifically: GSC impressions delta on the 8 COVER posts attributable to OG/Twitter card injection alone = 0 (within noise). The value of this work is latent — it pays out only when/if traffic from social channels arrives later.
rationale: Honest framing of leverage. OG tags affect rendering when URLs are shared on FB/X/LinkedIn/Slack/iMessage/Discord. None of those channels currently send GHP traffic. Google does not weight OG tags as ranking signals for organic SERP. So the prediction is the null hypothesis: zero short-term impact on the metric we are actually trying to move. If I cannot stake out this null prediction honestly, I am rationalizing.
action_taken: Injected og:image, og:image:alt, og:site_name, og:locale, twitter:card=summary_large_image, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image, twitter:image:alt across all 8 COVER posts via /tmp/inject_og_twitter.py. Reused the Amazon CDN image URL already in each Product schema.
